sentences.text: This chapter describes the RISC-V supervisor-level architecture, which contains a common core that is used with various supervisor-level address translation and protection schemes.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13

sentences.text: A number of CSRs are provided for the supervisor.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs

sentences.text: The `sstatus` register is an SXLEN-bit read/write register formatted as shown in when SXLEN=32 and when SXLEN=64.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The `sstatus` register keeps track of the processor's current operating state.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register

sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The SPP bit indicates the privilege level at which a hart was executing before entering supervisor mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When a trap is taken, SPP is set to 0 if the trap originated from user mode, or 1 otherwise.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When an SRET instruction (see ) is executed to return from the trap handler, the privilege level is set to user mode if the SPP bit is 0, or supervisor mode if the SPP bit is 1; SPP is then set to 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The SIE bit enables or disables all interrupts in supervisor mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When SIE is clear, interrupts are not taken while in supervisor mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When the hart is running in user-mode, the value in SIE is ignored, and supervisor-level interrupts are enabled.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The supervisor can disable individual interrupt sources using the `sie` CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The SPIE bit indicates whether supervisor interrupts were enabled prior to trapping into supervisor mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When a trap is taken into supervisor mode, SPIE is set to SIE, and SIE is set to 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When an SRET instruction is executed, SIE is set to SPIE, then SPIE is set to 1.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The `sstatus` register is a subset of the `mstatus` register.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The UXL field controls the value of XLEN for U-mode, termed UXLEN, which may differ from the value of XLEN for S-mode, termed SXLEN.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Base ISA Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: The encoding of UXL is the same as that of the MXL field of `misa`, shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Base ISA Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: When SXLEN=32, the UXL field does not exist, and UXLEN=32.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Base ISA Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: When SXLEN=64, it is a *WARL* field that encodes the current value of UXLEN.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Base ISA Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: In particular, an implementation may make UXL be a read-only field whose value always ensures that UXLEN=SXLEN.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Base ISA Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: If UXLEN!=SXLEN, instructions executed in the narrower mode must ignore source register operand bits above the configured XLEN, and must sign-extend results to fill the widest supported XLEN in the destination register.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Base ISA Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: If UXLEN < SXLEN, user-mode instruction-fetch addresses and load and store effective addresses are taken modulo 2^UXLEN^.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Base ISA Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: For example, when UXLEN=32 and SXLEN=64, user-mode memory accesses reference the lowest 4 GiB of the address space.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Base ISA Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: Some HINT instructions are encoded as integer computational instructions that overwrite their destination register with its current value, e.g., `c.addi x8, 0`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Base ISA Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: When such a HINT is executed with XLEN < SXLEN and bits SXLEN..XLEN of the destination register not all equal to bit XLEN-1, it is implementation-defined whether bits SXLEN..XLEN of the destination register are unchanged or are overwritten with copies of bit XLEN-1.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Base ISA Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: NOTE: This definition allows implementations to elide register write-back for some HINTs, while allowing them to execute other HINTs in the same manner as other integer computational instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Base ISA Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: The implementation choice is observable only by S-mode with SXLEN > UXLEN; it is invisible to U-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Base ISA Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: The MXR (Make eXecutable Readable) bit modifies the privilege with which loads access virtual memory.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Memory Privilege in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: When MXR=0, only loads from pages marked readable (R=1 in ) will succeed.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Memory Privilege in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: When MXR=1, loads from pages marked either readable or executable (R=1 or X=1) will succeed. MXR has no effect when page-based virtual memory is not in effect.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Memory Privilege in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: The SUM (permit Supervisor User Memory access) bit modifies the privilege with which S-mode loads and stores access virtual memory.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Memory Privilege in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: When SUM=0, S-mode memory accesses to pages that are accessible by U-mode (U=1 in ) will fault.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Memory Privilege in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: When SUM=1, these accesses are permitted. SUM has no effect when page-based virtual memory is not in effect, nor when executing in U-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Memory Privilege in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: Note that S-mode can never execute instructions from user pages, regardless of the state of SUM.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Memory Privilege in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: SUM is read-only 0 if `satp`.MODE is read-only 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Memory Privilege in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: The UBE bit is a *WARL* field that controls the endianness of explicit memory accesses made from U-mode, which may differ from the endianness of memory accesses in S-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Endianness Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: An implementation may make UBE be a read-only field that always specifies the same endianness as for S-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Endianness Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: UBE controls whether explicit load and store memory accesses made from U-mode are little-endian (UBE=0) or big-endian (UBE=1).
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Endianness Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: UBE has no effect on instruction fetches, which are implicit memory accesses that are always little-endian.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Endianness Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: For implicit accesses to supervisor-level memory management data structures, such as page tables, S-mode endianness always applies and UBE is ignored.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Endianness Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: Access to the `SPELP` field, added by Zicfilp, accesses the homonymous fields of `mstatus` when `V=0`, and the homonymous fields of `vsstatus` when `V=1`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Previous Expected Landing Pad (ELP) State in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: The S-mode-disable-trap (`SDT`) bit is a WARL field introduced by the Ssdbltrp extension to address double trap (See ) at privilege modes lower than M.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Double Trap Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: When the `SDT` bit is set to 1 by an explicit CSR write, the `SIE` (Supervisor Interrupt Enable) bit is cleared to 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Double Trap Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: This clearing occurs regardless of the value written, if any, to the `SIE` bit by the same write.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Double Trap Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: The `SIE` bit can only be set to 1 by an explicit CSR write if the `SDT` bit is being set to 0 by the same write or is already 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Double Trap Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: When a trap is to be taken into S-mode, if the `SDT` bit is currently 0, it is then set to 1, and the trap is delivered as expected.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Double Trap Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: However, if `SDT` is already set to 1, then this is an unexpected trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Double Trap Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: In the event of an unexpected trap, a double-trap exception trap is delivered into M-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Double Trap Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: To deliver this trap, the hart writes registers, except `mcause` and `mtval2`, with the same information that the unexpected trap would have written if it was taken into M-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Double Trap Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: The `mtval2` register is then set to what would be otherwise written into the `mcause` register by the unexpected trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Double Trap Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: The `mcause` register is set to 16, the double-trap exception code.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Double Trap Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: An `SRET` instruction sets the `SDT` bit to 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Status (`sstatus`) Register > Double Trap Control in `sstatus` Register

sentences.text: The `stvec` register is an SXLEN-bit read/write register that holds trap vector configuration, consisting of a vector base address (BASE) and a vector mode (MODE).
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Trap Vector Base Address (`stvec`)  Register

sentences.text: .Supervisor trap vector base address (`stvec`) register. include::images/bytefield/stvec.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Trap Vector Base Address (`stvec`)  Register

sentences.text: The BASE field in `stvec` is a field that can hold any valid virtual or physical address, subject to the following alignment constraints: the address must be 4-byte aligned, and MODE settings other than Direct might impose additional alignment constraints on the value in the BASE field.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Trap Vector Base Address (`stvec`)  Register

sentences.text: Note that the CSR contains only bits XLEN-1 through 2 of the address BASE.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Trap Vector Base Address (`stvec`)  Register

sentences.text: When used as an address, the lower two bits are filled with zeroes to obtain an XLEN-bit address that is always aligned on a 4-byte boundary.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Trap Vector Base Address (`stvec`)  Register

sentences.text: .Encoding of `stvec` MODE field. | |Value |Name |Description |0 + 1 + {ge}2 |Direct + Vectored |All exceptions set `pc` to BASE. + Asynchronous interrupts set `pc` to BASE+4{times}cause. + Reserved |
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Trap Vector Base Address (`stvec`)  Register

sentences.text: The encoding of the MODE field is shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Trap Vector Base Address (`stvec`)  Register

sentences.text: When MODE=Direct, all traps into supervisor mode cause the `pc` to be set to the address in the BASE field.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Trap Vector Base Address (`stvec`)  Register

sentences.text: When MODE=Vectored, all synchronous exceptions into supervisor mode cause the `pc` to be set to the address in the BASE field, whereas interrupts cause the `pc` to be set to the address in the BASE field plus four times the interrupt cause number.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Trap Vector Base Address (`stvec`)  Register

sentences.text: For example, a supervisor-mode timer interrupt (see ) causes the `pc` to be set to BASE+`0x14`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Trap Vector Base Address (`stvec`)  Register

sentences.text: Setting MODE=Vectored may impose a stricter alignment constraint on BASE.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Trap Vector Base Address (`stvec`)  Register

sentences.text: The `sip` register is an SXLEN-bit read/write register containing information on pending interrupts, while `sie` is the corresponding SXLEN-bit read/write register containing interrupt enable bits.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: Interrupt cause number i (as reported in CSR `scause`, ) corresponds with bit i in both `sip` and `sie`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits 15:0 are allocated to standard interrupt causes only, while bits 16 and above are designated for platform use.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: .Supervisor interrupt-pending register (`sip`). include::images/bytefield/sip.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: .Supervisor interrupt-enable register (`sie`). include::images/bytefield/sie.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: An interrupt i will trap to S-mode if both of the following are true: (a) either the current privilege mode is S and the SIE bit in the `sstatus` register is set, or the current privilege mode has less privilege than S-mode; and (b) bit i is set in both `sip` and `sie`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: These conditions for an interrupt trap to occur must be evaluated in a bounded amount of time from when an interrupt becomes, or ceases to be, pending in `sip`, and must also be evaluated immediately following the execution of an SRET instruction or an explicit write to a CSR on which these interrupt trap conditions expressly depend (including `sip`, `sie` and `sstatus`).
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: Interrupts to S-mode take priority over any interrupts to lower privilege modes.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: Each individual bit in register `sip` may be writable or may be read-only.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: When bit i in `sip` is writable, a pending interrupt i can be cleared by writing 0 to this bit.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: If interrupt i can become pending but bit i in `sip` is read-only, the implementation must provide some other mechanism for clearing the pending interrupt (which may involve a call to the execution environment).
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: A bit in `sie` must be writable if the corresponding interrupt can ever become pending.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits of `sie` that are not writable are read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: The standard portions (bits 15:0) of registers `sip` and `sie` are formatted as shown in Figures and respectively.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: .Standard portion (bits 15:0) of `sip`. include::images/bytefield/sipreg-standard.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: .Standard portion (bits 15:0) of `sie`. include::images/bytefield/siereg-standard.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits `sip`.SEIP and `sie`.SEIE are the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for supervisor-level external interrupts.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: If implemented, SEIP is read-only in `sip`, and is set and cleared by the execution environment, typically through a platform-specific interrupt controller.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits `sip`.STIP and `sie`.STIE are the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for supervisor-level timer interrupts.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: If implemented, STIP is read-only in `sip`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: When the Sstc extension is not implemented, STIP is set and cleared by the execution environment.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: When the Sstc extension is implemented, STIP reflects the timer interrupt signal resulting from `stimecmp`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: The `sip`.STIP bit, in response to timer interrupts generated by `stimecmp`, is set by writing `stimecmp` with a value that is less than or equal to `time`, and is cleared by writing `stimecmp` with a value greater than `time`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits `sip`.SSIP and `sie`.SSIE are the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for supervisor-level software interrupts.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: If implemented, SSIP is writable in `sip` and may also be set to 1 by a platform-specific interrupt controller.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: If the Sscofpmf extension is implemented, bits `sip`.LCOFIP and `sie`.LCOFIE are the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for local-counter-overflow interrupts. LCOFIP is read-write in `sip` and reflects the occurrence of a local counter-overflow overflow interrupt request resulting from any of the `mhpmeventn`.OF bits being set.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: If the Sscofpmf extension is not implemented, `sip`.LCOFIP and `sie`.LCOFIE are read-only zeros.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: Each standard interrupt type (SEI, STI, SSI, or LCOFI) may not be implemented, in which case the corresponding interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits are read-only zeros.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: All bits in `sip` and `sie` are *WARL* fields.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: The implemented interrupts may be found by writing one to every bit location in `sie`, then reading back to see which bit positions hold a one.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: Multiple simultaneous interrupts destined for supervisor mode are handled in the following decreasing priority order: SEI, SSI, STI, LCOFI.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Interrupt (`sip` and `sie`) Registers

sentences.text: Supervisor software uses the same hardware performance monitoring facility as user-mode software, including the `time`, `cycle`, and `instret` CSRs.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Timers and Performance Counters

sentences.text: The implementation should provide a mechanism to modify the counter values.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Timers and Performance Counters

sentences.text: The implementation must provide a facility for scheduling timer interrupts in terms of the real-time counter, `time`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Timers and Performance Counters

sentences.text: .Counter-enable (`scounteren`) register include::images/bytefield/scounteren.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Counter-Enable (`scounteren`) Register

sentences.text: The counter-enable (`scounteren`) CSR is a 32-bit register that controls the availability of the hardware performance monitoring counters to U-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Counter-Enable (`scounteren`) Register

sentences.text: When the CY, TM, IR, or HPMn bit in the `scounteren` register is clear, attempts to read the `cycle`, `time`, `instret`, or `hpmcountern` register while executing in U-mode will cause an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Counter-Enable (`scounteren`) Register

sentences.text: When one of these bits is set, access to the corresponding register is permitted.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Counter-Enable (`scounteren`) Register

sentences.text: `scounteren` must be implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Counter-Enable (`scounteren`) Register

sentences.text: However, any of the bits may be read-only zero, indicating reads to the corresponding counter will cause an exception when executing in U-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Counter-Enable (`scounteren`) Register

sentences.text: Hence, they are effectively *WARL* fields.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Counter-Enable (`scounteren`) Register

sentences.text: The `sscratch` CSR is an SXLEN-bit read/write register, dedicated for use by the supervisor.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Scratch (`sscratch`) Register

sentences.text: Typically, `sscratch` is used to hold a pointer to the hart-local supervisor context while the hart is executing user code.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Scratch (`sscratch`) Register

sentences.text: At the beginning of a trap handler, software normally uses a CSRRW instruction to swap `sscratch` with an integer register to obtain an initial working register.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Scratch (`sscratch`) Register

sentences.text: .Supervisor Scratch Register include::images/bytefield/sscratch.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Scratch (`sscratch`) Register

sentences.text: `sepc` is an SXLEN-bit read/write CSR formatted as shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Exception Program Counter (`sepc`) Register

sentences.text: The low bit of `sepc` (`sepc[0]`) is always zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Exception Program Counter (`sepc`) Register

sentences.text: On implementations that support only IALIGN=32, the two low bits (`sepc[1:0]`) are always zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Exception Program Counter (`sepc`) Register

sentences.text: If an implementation allows IALIGN to be either 16 or 32 (by changing CSR `misa`, for example), then, whenever IALIGN=32, bit `sepc[1]` is masked on reads so that it appears to be 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Exception Program Counter (`sepc`) Register

sentences.text: This masking occurs also for the implicit read by the SRET instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Exception Program Counter (`sepc`) Register

sentences.text: Though masked, `sepc[1]` remains writable when IALIGN=32.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Exception Program Counter (`sepc`) Register

sentences.text: `sepc` is a *WARL* register that must be able to hold all valid virtual addresses.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Exception Program Counter (`sepc`) Register

sentences.text: It need not be capable of holding all possible invalid addresses.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Exception Program Counter (`sepc`) Register

sentences.text: Prior to writing `sepc`, implementations may convert an invalid address into some other invalid address that `sepc` is capable of holding.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Exception Program Counter (`sepc`) Register

sentences.text: When a trap is taken into S-mode, `sepc` is written with the virtual address of the instruction that was interrupted or that encountered the exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Exception Program Counter (`sepc`) Register

sentences.text: Otherwise, `sepc` is never written by the implementation, though it may be explicitly written by software.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Exception Program Counter (`sepc`) Register

sentences.text: .Supervisor exception program counter register. include::images/bytefield/epcreg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Exception Program Counter (`sepc`) Register

sentences.text: The `scause` CSR is an SXLEN-bit read-write register formatted as shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Cause (`scause`) Register

sentences.text: When a trap is taken into S-mode, `scause` is written with a code indicating the event that caused the trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Cause (`scause`) Register

sentences.text: Otherwise, `scause` is never written by the implementation, though it may be explicitly written by software.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Cause (`scause`) Register

sentences.text: The Interrupt bit in the `scause` register is set if the trap was caused by an interrupt.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Cause (`scause`) Register

sentences.text: The Exception Code field contains a code identifying the last exception or interrupt. lists the possible exception codes for the current supervisor ISAs.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Cause (`scause`) Register

sentences.text: The Exception Code is a *WLRL* field.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Cause (`scause`) Register

sentences.text: It is required to hold the values 0–31 (i.e., bits 4–0 must be implemented), but otherwise it is only guaranteed to hold supported exception codes.
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Cause (`scause`) Register

sentences.text: .Supervisor Cause (`scause`) register. include::images/bytefield/scausereg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Supervisor-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Supervisor CSRs > Supervisor Cause (`scause`) Register
